extra.reason: Fixed: added ./tcproute.yaml + ./httproute.yaml to kubernetes/apps/databases/dolt/app/kustomization.yaml and applied both CRs to home cluster. bd connectivity restored via gateway IP.

Investigation: spegel-configuration init container already writes /etc/cri/conf.d/hosts/_default/hosts.toml on each node (log confirms: 'added containerd mirror configuration registry=//_default'). Talos v1.13 defaults containerd config_path to /etc/cri/conf.d/hosts so Spegel's per-registry hosts.toml files are honored automatically. Adding machine.registries.mirrors in Talos config would conflict with Spegel-managed config. No action needed — my original analysis was wrong.

Added systemReserved/kubeReserved (200m CPU, 512Mi mem, 2Gi ephemeral each), evictionHard (200Mi/10%/10%), containerLogMax{Size,Files} (50Mi/3), imageGC thresholds (75/60) to talos/patches/global/machine-kubelet.yaml. Rendered + validated in clusterconfig/.
Removed intel_iommu=on (wrong arch), replaced with amd_iommu=on + iommu.passthrough=1. vfio_pci module removed. Kernel args now live only in schematic.yaml (UKI required).
Resolved by moving kernel args into single source (schematic.yaml) since Talos v1.13 UKI refuses install.extraKernelArgs. machine-kernel.yaml now only holds (empty) modules list with comment pointer.
Changed amd_pstate=active → amd_pstate=guided so cpufreq.default_governor=performance actually takes effect.
Removed vm.nr_hugepages: 1024 sysctl. Reclaims ~2GB RAM per node (6GB cluster-wide). Verified no workloads request hugepages.
Removed nvme_tcp kernel module. No NVMe-over-TCP consumers in this cluster.
Pruned siderolabs/thunderbolt + siderolabs/iscsi-tools from schematic.yaml. New factory schematic ID 7ef48f8... uploaded and wired into talconfig.yaml for all 3 nodes.

Added nodeLabels per node inline in talconfig.yaml: node.kubernetes.io/cpu-tier (zen2 for bee-jms-01, zen3 for 02/03), node.kubernetes.io/memory-tier (standard for 01/02, high for 03). Used talhelper nodes[].nodeLabels field rather than per-node patch dirs.

Enabled machine.features.diskQuotaSupport: true. Removed apidCheckExtKeyUsage from plan — not a valid field in Talos v1.13 schema. Also removed stableHostname: true since it is already set by Talos defaults (was causing duplicate-key validation error).
Removed user.max_user_namespaces: 11255 magic value — kernel 6.18 default is sufficient.
Added Hardening Stance section to talos/patches/README.md documenting all perf-over-security trade-offs with rationale and restore path.

Flipped discard_unpacked_layers = false → true in machine-files.yaml. Safe now: verified Spegel-configuration init container writes /etc/cri/conf.d/hosts/_default/hosts.toml on each node, so containerd re-pulls hit P2P cache.
Resolved: after apply-node reboot cycle, all 3 nodes now report v1.36.0 matching talenv.yaml. Version drift gone.
MTU 9000 verified end-to-end from UDM gateway to all 3 k8s nodes + Synology NAS (192.168.35.5) with ping -M do -s 8972, 0% loss. Cilium status confirms 'MTU updated (9000)'. No MTU change needed.

admission-controller-patch.yaml was a no-op since Jan 2026 — strategic-merge 'admissionControl: []' did not override talhelper's default PodSecurity block. Verified by regenerating with and without the patch: identical rendered admissionControl (PodSecurity enforce=baseline, warn=restricted, audit=restricted, kube-system exempted). Deleted the file and removed reference from talconfig.yaml controlPlane.patches. Cluster continues to run with Talos default PodSecurity admission config, which is the correct posture.
Set bpf.masquerade: true and bpf.hostLegacyRouting: false in cilium values.yaml. Will take effect on next Flux reconcile + cilium rollout.

WONTFIX: inconsistent with documented homelab threat model (talos/patches/README.md). Cluster already runs with mitigations=off, security=none, apparmor=0, auditd disabled, secureboot off — trading security for perf on trusted LAN with no PHI/PCI workloads. Firewalling metrics ports is cosmetic relative to those accepted trade-offs. Revisit if cluster ever hosts external or regulated workloads.

All child issues resolved or closed with reason. 13 optimizations applied + verified live on all 3 nodes (schematic 7ef48f88, kernel args, kubelet reservations, node tier labels, Cilium full-eBPF datapath, diskQuotaSupport). 4 closed without code change (MTU verified clean, admission patch was no-op removed, k8s version caught up to v1.36.0, metrics firewall inconsistent with homelab threat model).

Added PSA privileged labels to monitoring namespace via kustomize patch on kubernetes/apps/monitoring/kustomization.yaml + applied live. node-exporter DS now 3/3 ready. Verified 'up{job="node-exporter"}' returns 1 for all 3 node instances (192.168.35.3/6/10 on :9100).

Updated CLAUDE.md Observability section: removed netdata entry, marked victoria-logs as 'disabled pending home-ops-d1e', noted prompp image override + migration plan under home-ops-v17, added Alertmanager + Tetragon entries to reflect reality.

VictoriaLogs 0.12.2 + bundled Vector DS deployed and ingesting. Chart upgraded 0.11.31→0.12.2 with schema rewrite (server.service.extraPorts, new route shape). Vector DS 3/3 ready, scraping /var/log/containers via kubernetes_logs source, shipping to VL via /insert/elasticsearch. Verified: 116k records ingested in first 2 minutes; fixed sink endpoint typo (victoria-logs → victoria-logs-server svc); stripped verbose node_labels/pod_labels/namespace_labels from every record (record size 7KB → 1.3KB). Existing Grafana GrafanaDatasource now resolves. Syslog listeners on :514 TCP/UDP preserved with existing TCPRoute + IngressRouteUDP.

Obsoleted by home-ops-d1e: the orphan PVC server-volume-victoria-logs-server-0 (50Gi) was reused by the re-deployed VictoriaLogs StatefulSet (fullnameOverride + chart naming produced matching STS/PVC name). Grafana victoria-logs GrafanaDatasource now resolves to the live service. No cleanup needed — PVC is now in active use.

VM operator 0.62.1 + VMSingle + VMAgent deployed and running parallel to Prometheus+Thanos. vm-operator converting all 45 PrometheusRules → VMRules and 33 ServiceMonitors → 35 VMServiceScrapes. VMAgent scraping 77 targets (Prom scrapes 76 — delta is VMSingle self-monitoring). up{job=node-exporter} returns 1 for all 3 nodes via VMSingle. Grafana vmsingle datasource wired at http://vmsingle-vmsingle.monitoring.svc.cluster.local:8429. Fixed 2 issues during rollout: Renovate mis-tagged OCI repo with app version (v0.69.0 → 0.62.1 chart version); serviceMonitor.vm defaulted true (chicken/egg with own CRD) → flipped to emit prom-operator ServiceMonitor. VMSingle pinned to bee-jms-03 (memory-tier=high), 100Gi openebs-hostpath PVC. 30d retention. Also incidentally raised Vector memory limits 512Mi → 1Gi after OOMKill at real log rate.

AM repeatInterval 12h→4h committed and live. Cilium envoy ServiceMonitor enabled — 3 envoy targets now scraped on port 9964 (all 3 nodes up=1) via both Prom and VMAgent. Skipped Dolt (no /metrics endpoint — port 8000 is h2c gRPC only), UPS (no hardware), Synology SMART (needs exporter on NAS, out of repo scope), and cert-manager cainjector/webhook (near-zero actionable metrics on homelab scale — can add later if churn appears).

Placeholder emails removed. Live middleware oidc-auth0-secure in network namespace now allowlists email:j0sh3rs@gmail.com only. Verified via kubectl after flux reconcile on commit a513dcf4. Functional auth flow validation deferred until first HTTPRoute references the middleware (rollout phase of epic home-ops-4gq).
Default App client 4bGOaPj4tK9G1xQRlt29UX3KhfFyYTyT deleted via dashboard 2026-04-29T19:57:03Z (confirmed via mgmt log sapi 'Delete a client' + list_applications shows only HomeOps + system meta).

Accepted current state per user decision 2026-04-29. Attack protection + refresh rotation gaps documented in session notes but deemed acceptable for homelab scope. Remaining hardening items (home-ops-sio callback tightening, home-ops-bxg role action, home-ops-pxi dragonfly auth, home-ops-d9o log stream, home-ops-tpl custom domain, home-ops-6b5 rotation runbook) deferred to post-Traefik-rollout phase.

Accepted current state per user decision 2026-04-29. Attack protection + refresh rotation gaps documented in session notes but deemed acceptable for homelab scope. Remaining hardening items (home-ops-sio callback tightening, home-ops-bxg role action, home-ops-pxi dragonfly auth, home-ops-d9o log stream, home-ops-tpl custom domain, home-ops-6b5 rotation runbook) deferred to post-Traefik-rollout phase.

Middleware rewritten to v0.8.1 plugin schema. Fields dropped: allowedUsersAndGroups, claimMapping, redirectURL, enableTokenValidation. Added: allowedUsers: [j0sh3rs@gmail.com], strictAudienceValidation: true, explicit logoutURL + postLogoutRedirectURI. Commit ddbccd9f. Flux reconciled. Live Middleware verified via kubectl jsonpath. Traefik pod logs show zero traefikoidc errors (plugin lazy-inits on first route, nothing wires in yet).

traefik-secrets had real client secret after user SOPS edit. Actual root cause was not placeholder — it was plugin behavior: traefikoidc does not expand ${VAR} placeholders in CRD spec strings. Switched to urn:k8s:secret:traefik-secrets:<key> native syntax. Closed placeholder narrative as stale.

Pilot working on tools.68cc.io. HTTP 302 redirect to https://68ccio.us.auth0.com/authorize verified with correct client_id + redirect_uri + scopes. Plugin logs show 'Fetching provider metadata' successfully. Remaining: full browser-based Google login flow test by user.

Closed as 'not needed — email allowlist sufficient'. Tenant is single-user (j0sh3rs@gmail.com via Google OIDC only). traefikoidc middleware uses allowedUsers for hard allowlist. Per-site role gates would require multi-user scenario. No Post-Login Action created — keeps Auth0 tenant at zero Actions (smaller attack surface, no Yaegi-plugin-like script trust). Revisit if/when a second identity joins the tenant. Noted: AUTH0_README.md still mentions role: syntax — follow-up docs cleanup tracked separately if we want it.

Runbook committed at docs/runbooks/auth0-rotation.md (commit on origin/main). Covers client_secret + session_encryption_key rotation procedures, Redis session purge, verification commands, rollback, audit-log check, and trigger conditions. Dry-run rotation not performed — deferred to actual scheduled rotation cycle.
